mnemonics: LeNet-5: 5 Layers of Fashion (7-layer architecture: C1, C3, S2, S4, C5, F6, Output)
C.A.S.H. Flow: Convolutions (C1, C3, C5), Subsampling (S2, S4), then Hidden fully-connected (F6) to Output
keyPoints: One of the earliest successful convolutional neural networks (CNNs), primarily used for handwritten digit recognition.
Pioneered the use of convolutional and subsampling (pooling) layers to achieve spatial invariance and reduce parameters.
commonMistakes: Assuming '5' refers to the total number of layers (it's 7, counting the input; '5' refers to the number of units with learnable parameters: C1, C3, C5, F6, Output).
Misidentifying the subsampling layers (S2, S4) as 'pooling layers' – they performed averaging, not max-pooling, and had trainable parameters.
